meaning: n.跳背游戏(分开两腿从背弯站立者身上跳过),[军](两支部队)交互跃进,竞相提高vi.跳背,交替前进vt.跃过
examples: The win allowed them to leapfrog three teams to gain second place.
The kids were playing leapfrog and doing somersaults in the backyard.
M + could leapfrog Kanazawa and become the Tate Modern of the East.
But the approval process at LeapFrog Enterprises , the Emeryville, Calif. maker of educational toys, comes close.
The process begins with a conversational free-for-all among a select 30 of LeapFrog's 250 employees.
